summary: Token efficiency is the key competitive axis for AI models — Anthropic leads on this metric
summary_extended: Baker argues that the two critical competitive variables determining which AI labs win economically are cost-per-token and token efficiency, and that these metrics will increasingly separate winners from losers in the foundation model market. His specific claim is that Anthropic can produce comparable-quality answers to Google using roughly half the tokens, giving it a structural cost and quality advantage. He also highlights xAI as having unusually low cost-per-token due to superior data center operations. The investment implication is that labs excelling on these two axes — Anthropic and xAI being his named examples — will dominate economically, while less efficient competitors will be squeezed. Neither Anthropic nor xAI is publicly listed, so there are no directly actionable US-listed equity plays that Baker unambiguously implies from this specific thesis.
detail: Baker argues that the sum of cost-per-token and token-efficiency is the critical competitive variable. Anthropic can produce comparable quality answers to Google using half the tokens. Combined with xAI's low cost-per-token from superior data center operations, these two metrics together determine which labs win economically.
confidence_evidence: Baker frames this as a structural thesis with specific mechanics, not a passing observation. He returns to it multiple times.
quote_fragment: token efficiency and cost per token is going to be really really important
